data.output:
---
ddx:
  id: SD-024
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-006
    - FEAT-022
    - SD-019
---
# Solution Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options

## Purpose

Eliminate the manual command-layer wiring pattern that copies config
fields into options structs at every dispatch site. Replace it with a
type-system-enforced contract where every worker, runner, and review
path receives a single resolved configuration object that the type
system requires. Make the bug class — "config field exists but no
caller wires it through, so the loop silently uses defaults" — locally
unreachable.

## Scope

In scope:

- Architectural pattern: `ResolvedConfig` (immutable) + per-family
  `*Runtime` structs replace the `*Options` family today.
- Three production dispatch sites that must call `config.Load`:
  CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`, GraphQL `StartWorker`.
- Five options families that get migrated in stages:
  `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`, `RunOptions`, `ExecuteBeadOptions`,
  `CompareOptions`, `QuorumOptions`.
- Three latent dead-config-field cases that surface during the
  refactor: `NoProgressCooldown`, `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, and
  `HeartbeatInterval` are hardcoded constants today; `ContextBudget`
  has no config home; `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` is parsed but never
  consumed.
- Lint enforcement (Stage 4) that mechanically forbids the old
  `*Options`-with-durable-fields pattern.
- Test coverage uplift: the four disabled config tests
  (`*.disabled`) get evaluated and either restored against the new
  API or deleted with rationale.

Out of scope:

- The CONTRACT-003 boundary with `~/Projects/agent`. Investigation
  confirmed the agent module does not import `ddx/internal/config`
  and ddx pre-resolves at `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:104`
  and `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:80`. No agent-repo changes
  are required and no contract document needs revision. Agent
  v0.9.9 (ADR-005 smart routing) further simplifies the boundary by
  removing `ServiceExecuteRequest.PreResolved` — `ResolveRoute` is
  now informational and `Execute` re-resolves on its own inputs.
  The ddx side just populates `EstimatedPromptTokens` and
  `RequiresTools` (new per-invocation hints) on `ServiceExecuteRequest`;
  routing decisions are owned upstream.
- Routing-layer cleanup driven by agent v0.9.9 (rendering composed
  inventory in `ddx agent route-status`, retiring `agent.routing.model_overrides`,
  dropping the "No model routes configured" fatal error path).
  Tracked separately as a small follow-up batch on top of FEAT-006;
  does not block this refactor and is not delivered by it.
- The wire shape of `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` /
  `agentlib.ExecuteResponse`. The refactor changes how ddx-side state
  is organized before the request is built; the request itself is
  unchanged.
- New CLI commands or operator workflows. This is a structural
  refactor; user-visible behavior is preserved.

## Problem

A reviewer recently caught that FEAT-022 Stage G shipped a
`ReviewMaxRetries` field on `*config.Config` plus a resolver, and an
`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.ReviewMaxRetries` field that the loop honors,
but no caller in `cli/cmd/` ever read the resolved value and assigned
it to the options struct. The config field was dead in production. The
test that claimed to verify the loop "respects" the override actually
injected the field directly into options.

Investigation found this is systemic, not a one-off:

- 6 production sites that construct `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`,
  `RunOptions`, or `ExecuteBeadOptions` across CLI and server.
  **Zero** of them call `config.Load` before constructing the options
  struct.
- Three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work` at
  `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1997`, server `runWorker` at
  `cli/internal/server/workers.go:878`, GraphQL `StartWorker` at
  `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:77`) all skip
  config entirely and populate options from CLI flags or request
  fields only. Every config-derived knob across every options struct
  is dead in the same way `ReviewMaxRetries` was.
- Two more `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` fields, `NoProgressCooldown` and
  `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, are hardcoded constants in
  `execute_bead_loop.go:292` with no config field at all. They have
  the same shape as `ReviewMaxRetries` would have had if no resolver
  existed.
- The Go type system permits constructing any `*Options` struct with
  zero-value defaults. The compiler cannot tell the implementer
  "you forgot to wire this field." Every new config knob is a new
  opportunity for the same bug.

ID:       ddx-0f3ae192
Title:    SD-024: Config-Driven Runtime Options
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 1
Labels:   ddx, kind:epic, area:config, area:agent, sd-024
Owner:    ddx
Desc:     Epic tracking SD-024 / TD-024 implementation: replace the *Options-struct-with-durable-fields pattern across 5 families (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions, RunOptions, ExecuteBeadOptions, CompareOptions, QuorumOptions) with type-system-enforced ResolvedConfig + *Runtime split.

Stages:
- Stage 1: Foundation + ExecuteBeadLoop migration (unblocks FEAT-022 G)
- Stage 2: RunOptions migration
- Stage 3: ExecuteBeadOptions migration
- Stage 4: Lint enforcement + test debt cleanup

See docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-024-config-driven-runtime-options.md
and docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-024-config-driven-runtime-options.md
for the design rationale and 35-bead inventory.
Accept:   All 35 child stage beads closed. runtimelint analyzer integrated into Lefthook + CI and passes on the post-cleanup tree. ExecuteBeadLoopOptions, RunOptions, ExecuteBeadOptions, CompareOptions, QuorumOptions no longer exist as types in cli/internal/agent/. ResolvedConfig usable via config.LoadAndResolve from all three dispatch sites (CLI, server worker, GraphQL StartWorker).

data.text:
All AC items verified clean:
- All 35 child stage beads closed (label sd-024 listing shows only the epic itself non-closed)
- `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`/`RunOptions`/`ExecuteBeadOptions`/`CompareOptions`/`QuorumOptions` no longer exist as types
- `config.LoadAndResolve` is wired from all three dispatch sites (CLI `cmd/agent_cmd.go`, server `workers.go:897`, GraphQL `resolver_feat008.go:103`)
- `runtimelint` analyzer exists at `tools/lint/runtimelint`, integrated into `lefthook.yml` (lines 171, 441), and passes clean against `./...`

This is an epic bead — its acceptance criteria are satisfied by the closed child beads, which have already been merged to the base. There's no further code change to make in this worktree, so a `no_changes_rationale.txt` is the correct outcome (avoiding the prior attempt's mistake of committing only execution evidence).

input.content:
Epic bead ddx-0f3ae192 (SD-024: Config-Driven Runtime Options) — no code diff required.

What is done
------------
All four acceptance criteria are satisfied by code already merged on the base
branch (cfc89b73) via the 35 closed child beads. Verified in this worktree:

1. All 35 child stage beads closed.
   `ddx bead list --label sd-024` shows every sd-024 bead in `closed` state
   except this epic itself.

2. ExecuteBeadLoopOptions / RunOptions / ExecuteBeadOptions / CompareOptions /
   QuorumOptions no longer exist as types in cli/internal/agent/.
   `grep -rn "ExecuteBeadLoopOptions\b|^type RunOptions|^type ExecuteBeadOptions|
   ^type CompareOptions|^type QuorumOptions" --include="*.go" cli/internal/agent/`
   returns zero matches.

3. config.LoadAndResolve usable from all three dispatch sites:
   - CLI `ddx work` / agent run / quorum / compare / execute-bead — `cmd/agent_cmd.go`
     lines 187, 245, 348, 1243, 1463, 1743, 2034 + `cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:119`.
   - Server `runWorker` — `internal/server/workers.go:897` (also :586 for retries).
   - GraphQL `StartWorker` — `internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:103`.

4. runtimelint analyzer integrated into Lefthook + CI and passes clean.
   - Source: `tools/lint/runtimelint/` and `tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint`.
   - Lefthook: `lefthook.yml:171` (pre-commit, staged-package scope) and
     `lefthook.yml:441` (full-tree gate).
   - Run: `go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...` exits 0
     with no output against the current tree.

Why there is nothing to commit
------------------------------
SD-024 is an epic; its implementation lives in the 35 child stage beads
(stages 1–4: Foundation, RunOptions migration, ExecuteBeadOptions migration,
Lint enforcement + test debt cleanup). Each child bead landed its own commit
on prior execute-bead runs and the work is already on the base revision this
worktree is rooted at. The epic bead is purely a roll-up — closing it does
not require new code, and committing execution-evidence-only would (and on
the previous attempt did) trip post-merge review with REVIEW:BLOCK because
no implementation diff appears against the epic.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Nothing in this worktree. The orchestrator should mark ddx-0f3ae192 closed
on the strength of the closed child beads + the AC verification above. If
post-merge review re-flags the epic for "no diff", the correct resolution is
to close the epic administratively rather than re-execute it as an
implementation bead.

data.text: Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting that all four AC items are already satisfied by the 35 closed child beads on the base revision, with concrete file:line evidence for each. This epic should be closed administratively rather than re-executed — committing execution-evidence-only against an epic was what tripped the prior REVIEW:BLOCK.
